title: Invocation: public fields without #[non_exhaustive] fossilizes 3-field layout
description: Invocation (jmap.rs) is pub with all fields pub and no #[non_exhaustive]. Test code already struct-initializes it directly (client.rs:577, 719, 823 etc). Once 0.1 ships, callers will depend on struct-literal construction. If the spec adds a 4th field to the [method,args,callId] tuple, adding it to Invocation is a breaking change for all call sites using struct literals. Adding #[non_exhaustive] now forces callers to use Invocation::new() — preventing struct literals — at the cost of requiring a small change to internal test helpers. Alternatively, document that Invocation::new() is the stable construction path and struct initialization is internal-only. Either way, the decision must be made before 0.1 is published.
